description_long: This is a military-style helicopter with a camouflage paint scheme, featuring a robust fuselage and a large main rotor with four long, straight blades. The helicopter is equipped with external weapon mounts and sensors, indicating its combat or tactical utility. The cockpit is located at the front, with multiple angular windows providing visibility for the crew. The tail section includes a smaller tail rotor mounted on the starboard side, and the landing gear consists of sturdy skids. The overall design is aerodynamic and functional, with various antennas and equipment visible on the exterior. The helicopter's size and build suggest it is capable of carrying several passengers or cargo, suitable for urban deployment or rapid response scenarios.
description: A camouflaged military helicopter with a four-blade main rotor, angular cockpit windows, external weapon mounts, and robust landing skids.
description_view_0: Front view showing the angular cockpit windows, main rotor blades extending outward, and the symmetrical landing skids.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the camouflage pattern, external weapon mounts, and the full length of the main and tail rotors.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and the symmetrical arrangement of the landing skids.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the helicopter's profile, including the cockpit, weapon mounts, and rotor configuration.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight main rotor blades made of composite material, each approximately 7-8 meters in length, colored dark gray or black with no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: A small tail rotor with four blades, mounted vertically on the starboard side of the tail boom, colored dark gray or black with no visible markings or protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: Two robust, tubular landing skids made of metal, colored dark gray or black, with crossbars for structural support and no visible footpads.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Multiple angular cockpit windows at the front, including two large forward-facing panes and several smaller side windows, all tinted and framed in black.
